report_name: ShinyHunters Leaks Data of 4.9M Charter Customers After Vishing Attack

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A cybercrime group that conducted a vishing attack against Charter Communications to compromise an employee's Microsoft Entra account, access a Salesforce instance, and exfiltrate customer data for extortion.
threat_actors.goals: To extort money from victim organizations by stealing and threatening to leak sensitive data.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Voice
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor initiated the attack with a voice phishing (vishing) call to a Charter Communications employee to deceive them into compromising their corporate credentials.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: ShinyHunters used stolen employee credentials to access Charter's corporate Microsoft Entra and Salesforce environments.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: The attackers used legitimate functionalities within the compromised Salesforce CRM platform to query and export a large volume of customer records.

campaigns.name: Salesforce Instance Targeting Campaign
campaigns.description: A wider campaign conducted by ShinyHunters targeting corporate Salesforce instances to exfiltrate data for extortion.

identities.name: Charter Communications
identities.description: A U.S. telecommunications provider, operating as Spectrum, that was breached by ShinyHunters, resulting in the leak of 4.9 million customer records from its Salesforce instance.

identities.name: Charter Communications Customers
identities.description: A group of 4.9 million customers whose data, including names, addresses, phone numbers, and account details, was exfiltrated from Charter Communications' Salesforce environment and leaked by ShinyHunters.

identities.name: Charter Communications Employees
identities.description: A group of 85,000 internal employees whose records were exposed in the data leak. An employee was the initial target of the vishing attack that led to the breach.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Data Leak Forum
infrastructure.description: A criminal forum where the ShinyHunters group leaked the stolen data from Charter Communications after the extortion attempt failed.

indicators.name: Anomalous Microsoft Entra Sign-in
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Microsoft Entra Sign-in Pattern
id: 7c5b9f8a-1e2d-4b3c-8a9f-0123456789ab
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects high-risk Microsoft Entra sign-in events, such as those related to impossible travel or unfamiliar locations, which could indicate a compromised cloud account as seen in the ShinyHunters campaign.
references:
  - https://securityaffairs.com/192892/uncategorized/shinyhunters-leaks-charter-communications-data-potentially-impacting-5-million-customers.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-01
tags:
  - attack.t1078.004
  - attack.initial_access
level: medium
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: signinlogs
detection:
  selection:
    RiskLevelDuringSignIn: 'high'
    RiskState: 'atRisk'
    RiskEventTypes|contains:
      - 'impossibleTravel'
      - 'unfamiliarSign_inProperties'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects anomalous sign-in patterns for Microsoft Entra accounts, such as impossible travel, which could indicate a compromised account as seen in the ShinyHunters attack on Charter Communications.

indicators.name: High Volume Salesforce API Query Activity
indicators.pattern:
title: High Volume Salesforce API Query Activity
id: 8d6c0a9b-2f3e-4c4d-9ba0-123456789abc
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects a high volume of API queries to Salesforce data endpoints, which could indicate data enumeration and exfiltration as performed by ShinyHunters. This rule is a hunting starting point and requires baselining and correlation in a SIEM.
references:
  - https://securityaffairs.com/192892/uncategorized/shinyhunters-leaks-charter-communications-data-potentially-impacting-5-million-customers.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-01
tags:
  - attack.t1213
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    cs-host|endswith: '.salesforce.com'
    c-uri|contains: '/services/data/'
    c-uri|contains|all:
      - '/query'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects an unusually high volume of API queries to Salesforce data endpoints, which could indicate data enumeration and exfiltration as performed by ShinyHunters.

relationships.source_name: High Volume Salesforce API Query Activity
High Volume Salesforce API Query Activity
